sentence_reviews.text: Entropy, mutual information, coding, and rate–distortion quantify information, compression, and uncertainty.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA newchap20.pdf lines 147-156 on information encoding, bits, and compression; Entropy (information theory) lines 224-230 on uncertainty, information, and lossless compression; Mutual information lines 173-175 on amount of information and dependence; Rate-distortion theory lines 144-150 on lossy compression and minimal bits per symbol.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources support each component concept, but no single source states the full sentence verbatim. The wording is a defensible synthesis of the cited material rather than an exact quotation.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Information theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA newchap20.pdf lines 19-22 and 147-156; Mutual information lines 173-175; Entropy (information theory) lines 224-230.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited sources support probability as the formal setting for random variables and mutual information, and AIMA explicitly ties probability to encoding and compression. The rationale is synthesized rather than directly quoted.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Information theory is modeled as depending on Probability theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Labels, targets, rewards & preferences, Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units, Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.

edge_reviews.notes: Probability theory is the correct prerequisite for the standard formulation of information theory, but the atlas rationale is a synthesis of AIMA's probability/encoding discussion and the information-theory definitions. The dependency direction is correct, and the edge strength is defensible as core.
